plugin.name: Apache Tomcat Maven Plugin :: Tomcat 7.x
plugin.description: The Tomcat Maven Plugin provides goals to manipulate WAR projects within the Tomcat 7.x servlet container.

mojo.description: Same as exec-war goal without forking the package lifecycle.

parameter.name: accessLogValveFormat
parameter.description: see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/valve.html

parameter.name: attachArtifact
parameter.description: Attach or not the generated artifact to the build (use true if you want to install or deploy it)

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifier
parameter.description: the classifier to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifierType
parameter.description: the type to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: connectorHttpProtocol
parameter.description: which connector protocol to use HTTP/1.1 or org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol

parameter.name: enableNaming
parameter.description: to enable naming when starting tomcat

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: File patterns to exclude from extraDependencies

parameter.name: extraDependencies
parameter.description: list of extra dependencies to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your jdbc driver, mail.jar etc.. Those dependencies will be in root classloader.

parameter.name: extraResources
parameter.description: list of extra resources to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your logger configuration etc

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the generated exec JAR.

parameter.name: httpPort
parameter.description: configure a default http port for the standalone jar

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: Main class to use for starting the standalone jar.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. The name to store webapp in exec jar. Do not use /

parameter.name: pluginWorkDirectory
parameter.description: Path under {@link #buildDirectory} where this mojo may do temporary work.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The maven project.

parameter.name: remoteRepos
parameter.description: List of Remote Repositories used by the resolver

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution

mojo.description: Deploy a WAR to Tomcat without forking the package lifecycle.

parameter.name: charset
parameter.description: The URL encoding charset to use when communicating with Tomcat manager.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat context XML file. This is not used for war deployment mode.

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: The deployment mode to use. This must be either war to deploy the war, context to deploy the context XML file, or both to deploy the war with the context XML file.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: password
parameter.description: The password to use for deployment

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: server
parameter.description: The server id in settings.xml to use when authenticating with Tomcat manager, or null to use defaults of username admin and no password.

parameter.name: tag
parameter.description: The Tomcat webapp tag name to use.

parameter.name: update
parameter.description: Whether Tomcat should automatically undeploy webapps that already exist when deploying.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The full URL of the Tomcat manager instance to use.

parameter.name: username
parameter.description: The tomcat username to use for deployment

parameter.name: warFile
parameter.description: The path of the WAR file to deploy.

mojo.description: Redeploy a WAR in Tomcat without forking the package lifecycle. (Alias for the deploy-only goal with its update parameter set to true.)

parameter.name: charset
parameter.description: The URL encoding charset to use when communicating with Tomcat manager.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat context XML file. This is not used for war deployment mode.

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: The deployment mode to use. This must be either war to deploy the war, context to deploy the context XML file, or both to deploy the war with the context XML file.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: password
parameter.description: The password to use for deployment

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: server
parameter.description: The server id in settings.xml to use when authenticating with Tomcat manager, or null to use defaults of username admin and no password.

parameter.name: tag
parameter.description: The Tomcat webapp tag name to use.

parameter.name: update
parameter.description: Whether Tomcat should automatically undeploy webapps that already exist when deploying.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The full URL of the Tomcat manager instance to use.

parameter.name: username
parameter.description: The tomcat username to use for deployment

parameter.name: warFile
parameter.description: The path of the WAR file to deploy.

mojo.description:
Display help information on tomcat7-maven-plugin.
Call mvn tomcat7:help -Ddetail=true -Dgoal=<goal-name> to display parameter details.

parameter.name: detail
parameter.description: If true, display all settable properties for each goal.

parameter.name: goal
parameter.description: The name of the goal for which to show help. If unspecified, all goals will be displayed.

parameter.name: indentSize
parameter.description: The number of spaces per indentation level, should be positive.

parameter.name: lineLength
parameter.description: The maximum length of a display line, should be positive.

mojo.description:
Shuts down all possibly started embedded Tomcat servers. This will be automatically done through a shutdown hook or you may call this Mojo to shut them down explictly.

By default the shutdown goal is not bound to any phase. For integration tests you might want to bind it to post-integration-test.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip execution

parameter.name: skipErrorOnShutdown
parameter.description: Ignore error when shutdown

mojo.description: Same as run-war goal without forking the package cycle.

parameter.name: addContextWarDependencies
parameter.description:
Will create a tomcat context for each dependencies of war type with 'scope' set to 'tomcat'. In other words, dependencies with: 
   <type>war</type>
   <scope>tomcat</scope>
To preserve backward compatibility it's false by default.

parameter.name: additionalConfigFilesDir
parameter.description: The directory contains additional configuration Files that copied in the Tomcat conf Directory.

parameter.name: address
parameter.description: this IP address will be used on all ports

parameter.name: ajpPort
parameter.description: The AJP port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The ajp connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.ajp.port

parameter.name: ajpProtocol
parameter.description:
The AJP protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's ajp. NOTE The ajp connector will be started only if {@link #ajpPort} > 0. possible values are:
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpProtocol - new blocking Java connector that supports an executor
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpAprProtocol - the APR/native connector.

parameter.name: aliases
parameter.description: configure aliases see Host Name aliases

parameter.name: backgroundProcessorDelay
parameter.description: represents the delay in seconds between each classPathScanning change invocation

parameter.name: classLoaderClass
parameter.description: Class loader class to set.

parameter.name: clientAuth
parameter.description: enable client authentication for https (if configured) see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_BIO_and_NIO

parameter.name: configurationDir
parameter.description: The directory to create the Tomcat server configuration under.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description:
The path of the Tomcat context XML file.

Since release 2.0, the file is filtered as a maven resource so you can use interpolation tokens ${ }

parameter.name: contextReloadable
parameter.description: Force context scanning if you don't use a context file with reloadable = "true". The other way to use contextReloadable is to add attribute reloadable = "true" in your context file.

parameter.name: defaultContextFile
parameter.description: The default context file to check for if contextFile not configured. If no contextFile configured and the below default not present, no contextFile will be sent to Tomcat, resulting in the latter's default context configuration being used instead.

parameter.name: fork
parameter.description: Set this to true to allow Maven to continue to execute after invoking the run goal.

parameter.name: hostName
parameter.description: configure host name

parameter.name: httpsPort
parameter.description: The https port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The https connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.https.port

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: jarScanAllDirectories
parameter.description: In case a module in your reactors has some web-fragments they will be read. If you don't need that for performance reasons, you can deactivate it.

parameter.name: keystoreFile
parameter.description: Override the default keystoreFile for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystorePass
parameter.description: Override the default keystorePass for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystoreType
parameter.description: Override the type of keystore file to be used for the server certificate. If not specified, the default value is "JKS".

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: port
parameter.description: The port to run the Tomcat server on. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.http.port

parameter.name: propertiesPortFilePath
parameter.description: Will dump port in a properties file (see ports for property names). If empty no file generated

parameter.name: protocol
parameter.description: The protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's HTTP/1.1. See possible values HTTP Connector protocol attribute

parameter.name: serverXml
parameter.description: server.xml to use Note if you use this you must configure in this file your webapp paths.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip execution

parameter.name: staticContextDocbase
parameter.description: The static context docroot base fully qualified path if null static context won't be added

parameter.name: staticContextPath
parameter.description: The static context

parameter.name: systemProperties
parameter.description: List of System properties to pass to the Tomcat Server.

parameter.name: tomcatLoggingFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat logging configuration.

parameter.name: tomcatUsers
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat users XML file.

parameter.name: tomcatWebXml
parameter.description: overriding the providing web.xml to run tomcat This override the global Tomcat web.xml located in $CATALINA_HOME/conf/

parameter.name: uriEncoding
parameter.description: The character encoding to use for decoding URIs.

parameter.name: useNaming
parameter.description:
Enables or disables naming support for the embedded Tomcat server.

Note: This setting is ignored if you provide a server.xml for your Tomcat. Instead please configure naming in the server.xml.

parameter.name: useSeparateTomcatClassLoader
parameter.description: if true a new classLoader separated from maven core will be created to start tomcat.

parameter.name: warDirectory
parameter.description: The path of the exploded WAR directory to run.

parameter.name: webapps
parameter.description: Collection of webapp artifacts to be deployed. Elements are <webapp> and contain usual GAVC plus contextPath and/or contextFile elements.

mojo.description: This Mojo will create an executable war file with embedded Tomcat that is also capable of being deployed elsewhere.

parameter.name: accessLogValveFormat
parameter.description: see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/valve.html

parameter.name: attachArtifact
parameter.description: Attach or not the generated artifact to the build (use true if you want to install or deploy it)

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifier
parameter.description: the classifier to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifierType
parameter.description: the type to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: connectorHttpProtocol
parameter.description: which connector protocol to use HTTP/1.1 or org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol

parameter.name: enableNaming
parameter.description: to enable naming when starting tomcat

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: File patterns to exclude from extraDependencies

parameter.name: extraDependencies
parameter.description: list of extra dependencies to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your jdbc driver, mail.jar etc.. Those dependencies will be in root classloader.

parameter.name: extraResources
parameter.description: list of extra resources to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your logger configuration etc

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the generated WAR.

parameter.name: httpPort
parameter.description: configure a default http port for the standalone jar

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: Main class to use for starting the standalone jar.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. The name to store webapp in exec jar. Do not use /

parameter.name: pluginWorkDirectory
parameter.description: Path under {@link #buildDirectory} where this mojo may do temporary work.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The maven project.

parameter.name: remoteRepos
parameter.description: List of Remote Repositories used by the resolver

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution

mojo.description: Runs the current project as a dynamic web application using an embedded Tomcat server.

parameter.name: addContextWarDependencies
parameter.description:
Will create a tomcat context for each dependencies of war type with 'scope' set to 'tomcat'. In other words, dependencies with: 
   <type>war</type>
   <scope>tomcat</scope>
To preserve backward compatibility it's false by default.

parameter.name: addWarDependenciesInClassloader
parameter.description: will add /WEB-INF/lib/*.jar and /WEB-INF/classes from war dependencies in the webappclassloader

parameter.name: additionalClasspathDirs
parameter.description: Additional optional directories to add to the embedded tomcat classpath.

parameter.name: additionalConfigFilesDir
parameter.description: The directory contains additional configuration Files that copied in the Tomcat conf Directory.

parameter.name: address
parameter.description: this IP address will be used on all ports

parameter.name: ajpPort
parameter.description: The AJP port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The ajp connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.ajp.port

parameter.name: ajpProtocol
parameter.description:
The AJP protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's ajp. NOTE The ajp connector will be started only if {@link #ajpPort} > 0. possible values are:
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpProtocol - new blocking Java connector that supports an executor
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpAprProtocol - the APR/native connector.

parameter.name: aliases
parameter.description: configure aliases see Host Name aliases

parameter.name: backgroundProcessorDelay
parameter.description: represents the delay in seconds between each classPathScanning change invocation

parameter.name: classLoaderClass
parameter.description: Class loader class to set.

parameter.name: clientAuth
parameter.description: enable client authentication for https (if configured) see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_BIO_and_NIO

parameter.name: configurationDir
parameter.description: The directory to create the Tomcat server configuration under.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description:
The path of the Tomcat context XML file.

Since release 2.0, the file is filtered as a maven resource so you can use interpolation tokens ${ }

parameter.name: contextReloadable
parameter.description: Force context scanning if you don't use a context file with reloadable = "true". The other way to use contextReloadable is to add attribute reloadable = "true" in your context file.

parameter.name: defaultContextFile
parameter.description: The default context file to check for if contextFile not configured. If no contextFile configured and the below default not present, no contextFile will be sent to Tomcat, resulting in the latter's default context configuration being used instead.

parameter.name: delegate
parameter.description: Set the "follow standard delegation model" flag used to configure our ClassLoader.

parameter.name: dependencies
parameter.description: The set of dependencies for the web application being run.

parameter.name: fork
parameter.description: Set this to true to allow Maven to continue to execute after invoking the run goal.

parameter.name: hostName
parameter.description: configure host name

parameter.name: httpsPort
parameter.description: The https port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The https connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.https.port

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: jarScanAllDirectories
parameter.description: In case a module in your reactors has some web-fragments they will be read. If you don't need that for performance reasons, you can deactivate it.

parameter.name: keystoreFile
parameter.description: Override the default keystoreFile for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystorePass
parameter.description: Override the default keystorePass for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystoreType
parameter.description: Override the type of keystore file to be used for the server certificate. If not specified, the default value is "JKS".

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: port
parameter.description: The port to run the Tomcat server on. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.http.port

parameter.name: propertiesPortFilePath
parameter.description: Will dump port in a properties file (see ports for property names). If empty no file generated

parameter.name: protocol
parameter.description: The protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's HTTP/1.1. See possible values HTTP Connector protocol attribute

parameter.name: serverXml
parameter.description: server.xml to use Note if you use this you must configure in this file your webapp paths.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip execution

parameter.name: staticContextDocbase
parameter.description: The static context docroot base fully qualified path if null static context won't be added

parameter.name: staticContextPath
parameter.description: The static context

parameter.name: systemProperties
parameter.description: List of System properties to pass to the Tomcat Server.

parameter.name: tomcatLoggingFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat logging configuration.

parameter.name: tomcatUsers
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat users XML file.

parameter.name: tomcatWebXml
parameter.description: overriding the providing web.xml to run tomcat This override the global Tomcat web.xml located in $CATALINA_HOME/conf/

parameter.name: uriEncoding
parameter.description: The character encoding to use for decoding URIs.

parameter.name: useNaming
parameter.description:
Enables or disables naming support for the embedded Tomcat server.

Note: This setting is ignored if you provide a server.xml for your Tomcat. Instead please configure naming in the server.xml.

parameter.name: useSeparateTomcatClassLoader
parameter.description: if true a new classLoader separated from maven core will be created to start tomcat.

parameter.name: useTestClasspath
parameter.description: will use the test classpath rather than the compile one and will add test dependencies too

parameter.name: warSourceDirectory
parameter.description: The web resources directory for the web application being run.

parameter.name: webapps
parameter.description: Collection of webapp artifacts to be deployed. Elements are <webapp> and contain usual GAVC plus contextPath and/or contextFile elements.

parameter.name: charset
parameter.description: The URL encoding charset to use when communicating with Tomcat manager.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat context XML file. This is not used for war deployment mode.

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: The deployment mode to use. This must be either war to deploy the war, context to deploy the context XML file, or both to deploy the war with the context XML file.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: password
parameter.description: The password to use for deployment

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: server
parameter.description: The server id in settings.xml to use when authenticating with Tomcat manager, or null to use defaults of username admin and no password.

parameter.name: tag
parameter.description: The Tomcat webapp tag name to use.

parameter.name: update
parameter.description: Whether Tomcat should automatically undeploy webapps that already exist when deploying.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The full URL of the Tomcat manager instance to use.

parameter.name: username
parameter.description: The tomcat username to use for deployment

parameter.name: warFile
parameter.description: The path of the WAR file to deploy.

mojo.description: This Mojo will create an executable war file with embedded Tomcat that is also capable of being deployed elsewhere.

parameter.name: accessLogValveFormat
parameter.description: see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/valve.html

parameter.name: attachArtifact
parameter.description: Attach or not the generated artifact to the build (use true if you want to install or deploy it)

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifier
parameter.description: the classifier to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifierType
parameter.description: the type to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: connectorHttpProtocol
parameter.description: which connector protocol to use HTTP/1.1 or org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol

parameter.name: enableNaming
parameter.description: to enable naming when starting tomcat

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: File patterns to exclude from extraDependencies

parameter.name: extraDependencies
parameter.description: list of extra dependencies to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your jdbc driver, mail.jar etc.. Those dependencies will be in root classloader.

parameter.name: extraResources
parameter.description: list of extra resources to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your logger configuration etc

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the generated WAR.

parameter.name: httpPort
parameter.description: configure a default http port for the standalone jar

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: Main class to use for starting the standalone jar.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. The name to store webapp in exec jar. Do not use /

parameter.name: pluginWorkDirectory
parameter.description: Path under {@link #buildDirectory} where this mojo may do temporary work.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The maven project.

parameter.name: remoteRepos
parameter.description: List of Remote Repositories used by the resolver

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution

parameter.name: charset
parameter.description: The URL encoding charset to use when communicating with Tomcat manager.

parameter.name: failOnError
parameter.description: Whether to fail the build if the web application cannot be undeployed.

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: password
parameter.description: The password to use for deployment

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: server
parameter.description: The server id in settings.xml to use when authenticating with Tomcat manager, or null to use defaults of username admin and no password.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The full URL of the Tomcat manager instance to use.

parameter.name: username
parameter.description: The tomcat username to use for deployment

mojo.description: Runs the current project as a packaged web application using an embedded Tomcat server.

parameter.name: addContextWarDependencies
parameter.description:
Will create a tomcat context for each dependencies of war type with 'scope' set to 'tomcat'. In other words, dependencies with: 
   <type>war</type>
   <scope>tomcat</scope>
To preserve backward compatibility it's false by default.

parameter.name: additionalConfigFilesDir
parameter.description: The directory contains additional configuration Files that copied in the Tomcat conf Directory.

parameter.name: address
parameter.description: this IP address will be used on all ports

parameter.name: ajpPort
parameter.description: The AJP port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The ajp connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.ajp.port

parameter.name: ajpProtocol
parameter.description:
The AJP protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's ajp. NOTE The ajp connector will be started only if {@link #ajpPort} > 0. possible values are:
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpProtocol - new blocking Java connector that supports an executor
  • org.apache.coyote.ajp.AjpAprProtocol - the APR/native connector.

parameter.name: aliases
parameter.description: configure aliases see Host Name aliases

parameter.name: backgroundProcessorDelay
parameter.description: represents the delay in seconds between each classPathScanning change invocation

parameter.name: classLoaderClass
parameter.description: Class loader class to set.

parameter.name: clientAuth
parameter.description: enable client authentication for https (if configured) see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_BIO_and_NIO

parameter.name: configurationDir
parameter.description: The directory to create the Tomcat server configuration under.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description:
The path of the Tomcat context XML file.

Since release 2.0, the file is filtered as a maven resource so you can use interpolation tokens ${ }

parameter.name: contextReloadable
parameter.description: Force context scanning if you don't use a context file with reloadable = "true". The other way to use contextReloadable is to add attribute reloadable = "true" in your context file.

parameter.name: defaultContextFile
parameter.description: The default context file to check for if contextFile not configured. If no contextFile configured and the below default not present, no contextFile will be sent to Tomcat, resulting in the latter's default context configuration being used instead.

parameter.name: fork
parameter.description: Set this to true to allow Maven to continue to execute after invoking the run goal.

parameter.name: hostName
parameter.description: configure host name

parameter.name: httpsPort
parameter.description: The https port to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's 0 this means won't be started. The https connector will be started only for value > 0. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.https.port

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: jarScanAllDirectories
parameter.description: In case a module in your reactors has some web-fragments they will be read. If you don't need that for performance reasons, you can deactivate it.

parameter.name: keystoreFile
parameter.description: Override the default keystoreFile for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystorePass
parameter.description: Override the default keystorePass for the HTTPS connector (if enabled)

parameter.name: keystoreType
parameter.description: Override the type of keystore file to be used for the server certificate. If not specified, the default value is "JKS".

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: port
parameter.description: The port to run the Tomcat server on. Will be exposed as System props and session.executionProperties with key tomcat.maven.http.port

parameter.name: propertiesPortFilePath
parameter.description: Will dump port in a properties file (see ports for property names). If empty no file generated

parameter.name: protocol
parameter.description: The protocol to run the Tomcat server on. By default it's HTTP/1.1. See possible values HTTP Connector protocol attribute

parameter.name: serverXml
parameter.description: server.xml to use Note if you use this you must configure in this file your webapp paths.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip execution

parameter.name: staticContextDocbase
parameter.description: The static context docroot base fully qualified path if null static context won't be added

parameter.name: staticContextPath
parameter.description: The static context

parameter.name: systemProperties
parameter.description: List of System properties to pass to the Tomcat Server.

parameter.name: tomcatLoggingFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat logging configuration.

parameter.name: tomcatUsers
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat users XML file.

parameter.name: tomcatWebXml
parameter.description: overriding the providing web.xml to run tomcat This override the global Tomcat web.xml located in $CATALINA_HOME/conf/

parameter.name: uriEncoding
parameter.description: The character encoding to use for decoding URIs.

parameter.name: useNaming
parameter.description:
Enables or disables naming support for the embedded Tomcat server.

Note: This setting is ignored if you provide a server.xml for your Tomcat. Instead please configure naming in the server.xml.

parameter.name: useSeparateTomcatClassLoader
parameter.description: if true a new classLoader separated from maven core will be created to start tomcat.

parameter.name: warDirectory
parameter.description: The path of the exploded WAR directory to run.

parameter.name: webapps
parameter.description: Collection of webapp artifacts to be deployed. Elements are <webapp> and contain usual GAVC plus contextPath and/or contextFile elements.

mojo.description: Redeploy a WAR in Tomcat. (Alias for the deploy goal with its update parameter set to true.)

parameter.name: charset
parameter.description: The URL encoding charset to use when communicating with Tomcat manager.

parameter.name: contextFile
parameter.description: The path of the Tomcat context XML file. This is not used for war deployment mode.

parameter.name: ignorePackaging
parameter.description: If set to true ignore if packaging of project is not 'war'.

parameter.name: mode
parameter.description: The deployment mode to use. This must be either war to deploy the war, context to deploy the context XML file, or both to deploy the war with the context XML file.

parameter.name: packaging
parameter.description: The packaging of the Maven project that this goal operates upon.

parameter.name: password
parameter.description: The password to use for deployment

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. This must always start with a forward-slash ('/').

parameter.name: server
parameter.description: The server id in settings.xml to use when authenticating with Tomcat manager, or null to use defaults of username admin and no password.

parameter.name: tag
parameter.description: The Tomcat webapp tag name to use.

parameter.name: update
parameter.description: Whether Tomcat should automatically undeploy webapps that already exist when deploying.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The full URL of the Tomcat manager instance to use.

parameter.name: username
parameter.description: The tomcat username to use for deployment

parameter.name: warFile
parameter.description: The path of the WAR file to deploy.

mojo.description: Create a self executable jar file containing all necessary Apache Tomcat classes. This allows for using just java -jar mywebapp.jar to run your webapp without needing to install a Tomcat instance. More details here.

parameter.name: accessLogValveFormat
parameter.description: see http://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/config/valve.html

parameter.name: attachArtifact
parameter.description: Attach or not the generated artifact to the build (use true if you want to install or deploy it)

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifier
parameter.description: the classifier to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: attachArtifactClassifierType
parameter.description: the type to use for the attached/generated artifact

parameter.name: connectorHttpProtocol
parameter.description: which connector protocol to use HTTP/1.1 or org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol

parameter.name: enableNaming
parameter.description: to enable naming when starting tomcat

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: File patterns to exclude from extraDependencies

parameter.name: extraDependencies
parameter.description: list of extra dependencies to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your jdbc driver, mail.jar etc.. Those dependencies will be in root classloader.

parameter.name: extraResources
parameter.description: list of extra resources to add in the standalone tomcat jar: your logger configuration etc

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the generated exec JAR.

parameter.name: httpPort
parameter.description: configure a default http port for the standalone jar

parameter.name: local
parameter.description: Location of the local repository.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: Main class to use for starting the standalone jar.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: The webapp context path to use for the web application being run. The name to store webapp in exec jar. Do not use /

parameter.name: pluginWorkDirectory
parameter.description: Path under {@link #buildDirectory} where this mojo may do temporary work.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The maven project.

parameter.name: remoteRepos
parameter.description: List of Remote Repositories used by the resolver

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution
